title: Beeswax floor polish paste
subtitle: A traditional beeswax paste for bare wood and stone floors
excerpt: Beeswax floor polish penetrates wood grain and stone pores, providing a low-sheen protective layer that resists water and foot traffic. It works on bare, untreated, or previously waxed wood floors and on natural stone tiles. Apply sparingly: too much wax builds up and becomes sticky.
sourceNotes: Beeswax floor polish formula from Traditional Leatherworking, Waterer (1981). Application technique from the Guild of Traditional Floor Polishing guide. Lavender EO properties from Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014).

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally for larger batches. A 200 g tin covers a medium-sized room floor with one application.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The paste keeps for 2 years. Make ahead and store in a sealed jar. Apply 2 to 3 times per year to maintained floors, more frequently to new or stripped floors.
recipe.temperatureNote: Melt beeswax over a water bath to around 65 degrees Celsius. Stir in jojoba and essential oils off the heat to prevent volatilisation of the fragrance notes.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 2 years in a sealed jar. No water content means no preservative required. The paste will harden in cold temperatures and soften in warm ones: both are normal. Work from room temperature paste for the easiest application.

recipeTools.notes: Or a heat-proof jug; dedicated to non-food use

glossaryTerms.definition: The accumulation of excess wax on a floor surface from repeated applications without removal of the previous layer. Wax buildup turns floors yellowish and sticky, and the surface loses its shine. Prevent by applying only a very thin layer at each session. Strip old buildup with a commercial wax stripper or white spirit before re-waxing.

content.text: The most common mistake with beeswax floor polish is applying too much. A thin, even layer applied with a cloth and buffed to a shine after 30 minutes gives a better result than a thick application.
is the primary failure mode with wax-polished floors. Apply annually or twice-yearly to maintained floors: not monthly.

This polish is suitable for bare, oiled, or previously waxed wood floors and for natural stone tiles. Do not use on lacquered or varnished floors, which have a surface coating that wax cannot penetrate. Do not use on laminate flooring. The freshly applied floor will be slippery until the wax has been buffed and dried: keep children and pets off the area until dry.

items.prepNote: Penetrating carrier oil; conditions the wood or stone
5% of formula; fragrance and mild antimicrobial

content.text: Melt the beeswax in the jug over a simmering-water bath until fully liquid. Remove from heat. Stir in the jojoba oil, then the lavender essential oil. Pour into a wide-mouth jar. Leave to set undisturbed at room temperature for 1 hour.

To use: clean and dry the floor first. Apply a very thin layer of polish to a lint-free cloth. Work in sections, rubbing the wax into the floor with circular movements. Leave for 30 minutes. Buff to a low shine with a clean cloth or a floor buffer. The floor will be slightly slippery until fully dried and buffed.
2 years in a sealed jar. Store at room temperature. If the paste has hardened, warm the closed jar briefly in warm water and stir before use.
